content.problemStatement:
Dhinwaji is an acclaimed poet and likes to play with words and letters. He has bought some stickers where each sticker has a lower case english letter (a-z). The letters are indexed from 1 - 26 i.e. `a` has index 1, `b` has index 2 and so on. He has aia_iai​ stickers with letter iii on it. He needs to create a new word having exactly nnn letters. Being a programmer, Dhinwaji imposed another constraint: a letter with index jjj can only be placed at position iii in the word if i%j=0i \% j = 0i%j=0 i.e. iii should be a multiple of jjj. Note that everything is 1-indexed. Note also that not all the stickers need to be used.

Dhinwaji wonders what is the lexicographically smallest word he can create that follows the above constraints. Since he is too busy writing poems, you have to help him find this word. Note that it is possible that there is no valid word of nnn letters that can be formed.


Input Format

The first line will have a number TTT indicating the number of test cases. TTT lines follow.
The first line of each test case will have one integer, nnn, denoting the required length of the new word.
The second line of each test case will have 26 space separated integers a1a_1a1​, a2a_2a2​, ..., a26a_{26}a26​.


Output Format

For each test case, print one line containing the lexicographically smallest word which satisfies the conditions. If no such word is possible, print `#rekt` without the quotes.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Testcase 1: There is 1 sticker with a, b and c each. "abc" is the only valid word possible.
Testcase 2: Some valid words are "abcaab", "abcbaa", "ababac" etc. The lexicographically smallest among them is aacbab.
Testcase 3: There are a total of 3 letters so a word with 10 letters cannot be formed.
